fuzz_deployNewSupertoken((uint8,uint8,ui.custom:property: supertoken total supply starts at zero

fuzz_relayERC20(uint256).custom:property: relayERC20 increases the token's totalSupply in the destination chain exactly by the input amountrelayERC20 increases sender's balance in the destination chain exactly by the input amountcalls to relayERC20 always succeed as long as the cross-domain caller is valid

fuzz_deployNewSupertoken((uint8,uint8,ui.notice: deploy a new supertoken with deploy salt determined by params, to the given (of course mocked) chainId

handler_mintSupertoken(uint256,uint96).notice: pick one already-deployed supertoken and mint an arbitrary amount of it necessary so there is something to be bridged :D TODO: will be replaced when testing the factories and `convert()`

handler_supERC20Transfer(uint256,uint256.notice: The ToB properties don't preclude the need for this since they always use address(this) as the caller, which won't get any balance until it's transferred to it somehow
